heroPoems.title: On Giving
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Nothing Is Lost
heroPoems.firstStanza:
No effort on this path is wasted.
No sincere move is erased.

heroPoems.title: O sadhu! the simple union is the best. Since the day when I met
Lines on the Mermaid Tavern
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Souls of Poets dead and gone,
What Elysium have ye known,
Happy field or mossy cavern,
Choicer than the Mermaid Tavern?
Have ye tippled drink more fine
Than mine host's Canary wine?

heroPoems.title: Mr. Apollinax
heroPoems.firstStanza:
When Mr. Apollinax visited the United States
His laughter tinkled among the teacups.
I thought of Fragilion, that shy figure among the birch-trees,
And of Priapus in the shrubbery
Gaping at the lady in the swing.
In the palace of Mrs. Phlaccus, at Professor Channing-Cheetah's
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: No one in the taverns is as mad as I am
heroPoems.firstStanza:
No one in the taverns is as mad as I am
My robe lies pawned for wine
my book lies pawned beside it
both holiness and shame spent in one night

heroPoems.title: Love made me poor in every useful way.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Love made me poor in every useful way.
I can no longer pass for practical.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: The Heavens Declare
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The heavens declare the glory of God,
and the firmament shows his handiwork.
Day to day pours out speech,
and night to night reveals knowledge.

heroPoems.title: Sonnets to Orpheus II, 1
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Breathing, you invisible poem.
Again and again exchanged in pure space.
Counterweight in which I happen rhythmically.
Single wave whose gradual sea I am.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: My desires are many and my cry is
heroPoems.firstStanza:
My desires are many and my cry is pitiful, but ever didst thou save me
by hard refusals; and this strong mercy has been wrought into my life
through and through.
Day by day thou art making me worthy of the simple, great gifts that
thou gavest to me unasked—this sky and the light, this body and the
life and the mind—saving me from perils of overmuch desire.
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

collections.name: A Narrow Bridge of Air
collections.theme: A shelf of poems for evenings when ordinary objects feel quietly charged with meaning.

featuredPoem.title: My Lord hides Himself, and my Lord wonderfully reveals Himself
featuredPoem.firstLines:

My Lord has encompassed me with hardness, and my Lord has cast

collections.name: Where Letters Grow Quiet
collections.theme: Poems that stay close to thresholds, departures, and the soft mechanics of return.

featuredPoem.title: Architecture of Food and Faith
featuredPoem.firstLines:
What you eat,
what you praise,

collections.name: The Grammar of Ash and Light
collections.theme: An inward territory of lamplight, weather, and private observation.

poems.title: The song that I came to sing remains
Knowledge, Work, and the Inner Fire
How could I sleep in this moonlight?
This is my prayer to thee, my lord
